title: PM Lane 194 - PM Intake Field Start Source Review Bring-Back Lens
authority: repo technical authority; PM lane implementation authority; final integration authority
objective: Expose a compact, phone-first, browser-local field-start source review bring-back lens inside the PM intake workbench so Jason can decide whether returned drawings, workbook rows, site notes, observer/source context, or work-area references need source review before any later bounded packet without creating meeting notes, tasks, action items, owners, due dates, commitments, reports, routes, storage keys, exports, hosted calls, or write paths.
scope: Added a `Local Field Start Source Review Bring-Back Lens` section inside the existing `Local Field Start Customer/Site Questions Quick Review` panel on `/pm-review/import-intake`.
Placed the section immediately after the PM Lane 193 bring-back review queue as a sibling section so Lane 190 through Lane 193 proof remains unchanged.
Derived five linked source review cards from the existing candidate, field questions, and field observation scratchpad state.
Covered drawing/workbook source check, site note source check, observer/source context check, work-area reference check, and source review packet boundary.
Kept the section links/cards only: no buttons, no meeting-note capture, no export action, no text inputs, no persisted disclosure state, no new localStorage key, and no backend route.

Extended the focused read-only PM import-intake smoke to prove desktop presence, `390x844` mobile visibility, initial and populated source lens states, five links, zero buttons, no source review lens storage key, no implied-authority link/control text, and zero mutation calls.

Used a read-only sidecar to confirm the safest sibling placement, five-item source lens shape, copy-risk boundary, and no-write assertion set while VS Code Codex retained PM implementation and final integration authority.
guardrails: No Supabase, Render, Vercel, or Olares product action.
No browser approval button, approval POST wiring, approval submission, or approval-row creation.
No new export action, filename, payload version, localStorage schema, backend route, handler, service/auth/ingress change, or autonomous AI business-state mutation.

No meeting-note capture, task, action item, owner field, due date field, issue, field authorization, lead or crew assignment, schedule/status write, customer commitment, customer report, durable field record, production tracking, completion evidence, billing, payroll, invoice, accounting, or external finance-system write.
validation: rg -n "PM Lane 194|Local Field Start Source Review Bring-Back Lens|fieldStartSourceReviewBringBackLens|pm-field-start-source-review-bring-back-lens|source review lens|sourceReviewBringBackLens|mutationRequests\)\.toHaveLength\(0\)" PROJECT_STATUS.md docs/operations ops/agents apps/operations-web
Null-byte check on PM status docs
validation_result: PASS - operations-web typecheck passed; operations-web production build passed with /pm-review/import-intake in the route output; focused Playwright pm import-intake smoke passed cleanly with the Local Field Start Source Review Bring-Back Lens, 390x844 mobile proof, five source lens links, zero buttons, no source review lens storage key, no implied-authority link/control text, and zero mutation calls; packet JSON parsed as 2026-05-17-pm-lane-194; PM Lane 194 guardrail rg passed; null-byte check passed on PM status docs; git diff --check passed with only expected LF-to-CRLF warnings on touched and unrelated files.
sidecar_result: Read-only sidecar Poincare recommended placing the source review bring-back lens immediately after the bring-back review queue inside the customer/site questions panel, deriving five source-review checks from candidate, field question, and observation context, avoiding meeting-note or accountability language, adding no state/export/route/write path, and proving no storage keys or mutation calls.
